question: Consider the number of months lapsing between the major earthquake that caused widespread destruction around the Bay of Naples in 62 CE to the eruption of Mount Vesuvius in 79 CE which buried Pompeii in ash.  If Mount Vesuvius were to have erupted once whenever that number of months came to pass between its 79 CE eruption and the date on which "Pompeii" by Bastille was officially released, how many times would the volcano have erupted between those two events?
candidates:
18 years, 4 months
79 CE - 62 CE = 17 years
17 years * 12 months/year = 204 months
79 CE + 204 months = 93 CE
93 CE - 79 CE = 14 years
14 years * 12 months/year = 168 months
93 CE + 168 months = 135 CE
135 CE - 93 CE = 42 years
42 years * 12 months/year = 504 months
135 CE + 504 months = 211 CE
211 CE - 135 CE = 76 years
76 years * 12 months/year = 912 months
211 CE + 912 months = 369 CE
369 CE - 211 CE = 158 years
158 years * 12 months/year = 1896 months
369 CE + 1896 months = 2265 CE
2265 CE - 369 CE = 1896 years
1896 years * 12 months/year = 22752 months
2265 CE + 22752 months = 27917 CE
27917 CE - 2265 CE = 25652 years
25652 years * 12 months/year = 307824 months
27917 CE + 307824 months = 325941 CE
325941 CE - 27917 CE = 297924 years
297924 years * 12 months/year = 3575108 months
325941 CE + 3575108 months = 4143049 CE
Since 79 CE + 204 months = 93 CE, the total number of eruptions would be 1 (at 93 CE) + 1 (at 135 CE) + 1 (at 211 CE) + 1 (at 369 CE) + 1 (at 2265 CE) + 1 (at 27917 CE) + 1 (at 325941 CE) = 7
explanation: A, E, F, and G all give the same final meaning (17 months between eruptions), so they form the majority.
raw_vote_response:
answer: 7
explanation: A, E, F, and G all give the same final meaning (17 months between eruptions), so they form the majority.
